labels.65: The feature activates on text transitions where a positive development, improvement, or hopeful resolution follows a challenge or difficult situation.
labels.132: The feature fires on text immediately following a noun or noun phrase, particularly at the boundary where descriptive context or location information begins.
labels.225: Phrases expressing availability or potential for action, often using words like "opportunity," "chance," or "will" in contexts of future possibility.
labels.248: Phrases that complete or follow a statement about providing, delivering, or achieving something beneficial.
labels.273: Text fragments appearing between quoted markers that introduce new clauses or topics following a comma or conjunction.
labels.279: Text phrases describing prerequisites, conditions, or contextual details about activities, courses, or services.
labels.280: Phrases describing professions or roles, often paired with names or descriptions of what people do.
labels.287: Phrases listing two complementary categories or scopes connected by "and" (e.g., residential and commercial, short and long).
labels.313: Transitions or shifts between contrasting ideas, states, or time periods marked by conjunctions or discourse markers.
labels.2065: Phrases indicating a time period or duration of change/impact ("has been/has" + temporal reference).
labels.2066: The feature activates on numbers appearing in mathematical expressions, equations, measurements, or numerical comparisons.
labels.2067: Proper nouns or brand names being inserted or introduced in the middle of text.
labels.2079: The feature activates on proper nouns of local businesses, farms, organizations, and institutions.
labels.2085: Technical skill or tool proficiency descriptions using passive voice or institutional/organizational context.
labels.2123: Recent past time expressions (last week/month/year, yesterday, past Tuesday) indicating when events occurred.
labels.2166: The feature detects "good" or "good X" phrases and positive evaluative language used in comparative or descriptive contexts.
labels.2183: The feature activates on phrases describing geographic, spatial, or categorical locations introduced by prepositions like "on," "at," or "in."
labels.2260: Capitalized proper nouns or titles that appear immediately after line breaks or section breaks in formatted text.
labels.2280: Phrase "actually" or "possibly" used as filler words between unrelated clauses in awkwardly constructed sentences.
labels.2304: Mentions of specific days of the week or time periods (weekend, Saturday, Sunday, Memorial Day) that indicate when events occur.
labels.2324: Text spans where quoted or parenthetical clarifications interrupt the main sentence flow.
labels.2340: Attribution of quoted statements to people by their name and professional title or affiliation.
labels.2378: The feature activates on short noun phrases or descriptive pairs separated by commas that elaborate or extend a preceding concept.
labels.2380: The feature fires on quantity measurements (numbers, fractions, units) in recipe ingredient lists.
labels.4107: Phrases describing obstacles, barriers, or things standing between two opposing states or conditions.
labels.4124: The feature activates on phrases where "is an" introduces a definition or explanation of a noun.
labels.4183: Words in parentheses that are maiden names or alternate name forms.
labels.4227: Comparative or contrastive statements using "about," "than," "in," or "but" to introduce a contrasting clause or alternative perspective.
labels.4240: Transition between clauses or sentences describing someone's willingness to take on challenging roles despite their prominence.
labels.4255: Phrases expressing "to have/gain/be comfortable with" followed by abstract concepts or outcomes that come after a preposition or connector.
labels.4278: Contractions or abbreviated words split across token boundaries (e.g., "couldn't", "he's", "I've", "You'll").
labels.4334: Obituary or genealogical text containing family names, relationships, and biographical details of deceased individuals.
labels.4347: Transitions between topics or sentences marked by temporal, spatial, or logical connectors.
labels.4382: The feature activates on phrases describing personal interactions, social situations, or encounters between people.
labels.4400: Transitional phrases or conjunctions connecting two related clauses or ideas within sentences.
labels.6148: Proper nouns and titles appearing after dashes or at the boundary of document metadata/headers.
labels.6152: Phrases describing time periods or durations within a larger timespan or context.
labels.6180: Acronyms or abbreviated terms appearing in parentheses immediately after their full expanded form.
labels.6204: Contrasting ideas or shifting perspectives using "but", "however", or similar adversative conjunctions.
labels.6232: Four-digit years from 2007-2018 appearing at the end of date headers in blog posts or articles.
labels.6256: Wedding photography service descriptions and promotional content about capturing couples' special moments.
labels.6334: Phrases describing physical objects or spaces being placed, positioned, or located in specific areas.
labels.6376: Phrases introducing temporal context or recent events with descriptive clauses.
labels.6438: Passive voice or past participle constructions followed by prepositional phrases indicating location, purpose, or time.
labels.6479: Passive voice or past tense constructions describing completed actions or states.
labels.8211: Parsing or extracting structured data and metadata fields from text.
labels.8245: Text passages containing parenthetical explanations or clarifying phrases that provide context or examples.
labels.8253: Phrases discussing problems, solutions, or outcomes that appear between contrasting or pivotal statements.
labels.8264: Text segments that are split or interrupted mid-word or mid-phrase by formatting markers or product/proper name boundaries.
labels.8290: Career transitions or professional role descriptions introducing someone's work experience and expertise.
labels.8322: Transitional phrases introducing articles, sections, or content ("In this article/post/page", "For reference", "See More").
labels.8328: The feature activates on phrases describing properties, characteristics, or qualities between adjectives and nouns or verbs and objects.
labels.8416: Time stamps in 24-hour or 12-hour format with colons separating hours, minutes, and seconds.
labels.8465: The feature activates on phrases where "the" precedes a noun or noun phrase that follows a preposition or verb.
labels.8474: Repetitive actions or processes that are avoided or eliminated through a solution or feature.
labels.8475: Sentences containing interjections, exclamations, or casual emotional expressions within the highlighted tokens.
labels.10381: Text transitions where the feature fires at sentence or clause boundaries, particularly before quoted speech or new independent clauses.
labels.10383: Empty or whitespace-only token sequences between markers.
labels.10400: The feature detects the phrase "available" followed by a preposition or descriptor indicating availability conditions or options.
labels.10410: Feature fires on month/time period names followed by years or temporal context.
labels.10537: Phrases describing volunteer efforts, community involvement, or organizational achievements in promotional or descriptive contexts.
labels.10557: Phrases describing progress, improvement, or positive change in a situation or outcome.
labels.10563: Phrases describing receiving or getting something positive, often preceded by "a" or "an" article and followed by descriptive feedback or outcomes.
labels.12293: Conversational phrases or transitions introducing a new topic or shift in thought.
labels.12316: The feature identifies proper names (first and last names) of people positioned between the activating tokens.
labels.12318: Consecutive verb phrases or actions connected by "and" describing what an entity does or produces.
labels.12334: Education sector-related nouns and phrases describing school types, educational programs, or learning environments.
labels.12350: Instructions or prompts for downloading files or software.
labels.12357: The feature fires on periods or punctuation marks followed by author names, institutional affiliations, or the beginning of new sentences in academic and professional text.
labels.12364: Comparative or conditional phrases introducing what follows, often using "with" or "to" to set up explanations or examples.
labels.12389: Transition words or phrases connecting consequences to their causes or explanations.
labels.12392: Dates and times of upcoming events or scheduled activities.
labels.12445: Tokens following a dash, colon, or punctuation that specify measurements, dimensions, or categorical specifications.
labels.12453: Age or age-related descriptors separated from adjacent text by punctuation or formatting.
labels.12472: Phrases transitioning from a topic introduction to discussion or questioning about that topic.
labels.12479: Personal activities or experiences the writer engaged in or did during a specific time period.
labels.12515: The feature activates on text containing parenthetical abbreviations or acronyms in parentheses immediately after their full form.
labels.12533: Temporal or numerical information (dates, times, durations) enclosed or modified by surrounding phrases.
labels.12597: Time representations in timestamps showing digits that form the minutes portion (appearing between hour and AM/PM or timezone indicators).
labels.14336: Transition words or phrases connecting two related clauses or sentences.
labels.14395: Feature activates on phrases offering alternative options or solutions introduced by conjunctions like "can," "or," or "want to."
labels.14396: The feature detects transitions between clauses or sentences where a contrasting idea or new assertion follows a period or conjunction.
labels.14422: The feature activates on text discussing social gatherings and occasions like parties, weddings, events, and receptions.
labels.14482: Negation or denial of necessity/requirement expressed through phrases like "no need," "don't need," or "less of a reason."
labels.14501: Parenthetical or clarifying asides inserted between main clauses using "that" or similar connectors.
labels.14519: Text transitions between topic shifts or section boundaries marked by punctuation and formatting changes.
labels.14540: Capitalized words or numbers appearing at the beginning of phrases or list items.
labels.14627: Feature activates on city names followed by state abbreviations in news article datelines.
labels.14653: The feature fires on text describing physical or technical attributes that are enclosed or emphasized between punctuation marks or clause boundaries.
labels.16385: Transitional phrases introducing new information or elaboration within sentences.
labels.16409: The feature activates on phrases introducing a notable fact, characteristic, or consequence that follows a setup clause.
labels.16424: The feature fires on acronyms or abbreviations in parentheses that immediately follow their expanded form.
labels.16426: Text discussing student-related topics or student services/benefits with descriptive context.
labels.16503: Conjunctions or transitional phrases connecting independent clauses or ideas within sentences.
labels.16521: Phrases connecting two related clauses or ideas with emphasis on what follows the connector.
labels.16575: The feature activates on text segments containing common phrases or clauses that form natural breaks between related ideas or explanations.
labels.16583: The feature fires on temporal or sequential markers indicating the beginning or start of something (season, year, game, event).
labels.16697: The feature activates on transitions between two sentences or clauses separated by a period or conjunction.
